snakes.name: Indian Cobra
snakes.behavior: Aggressive when threatened, rears up and spreads iconic hood as a warning display.
snakes.facts: Sacred in Hindu mythology and a symbol of power. Venom contains powerful post-synaptic neurotoxins causing respiratory failure.

snakes.name: Russell's Viper
snakes.behavior: Coils tightly, hisses loudly as a warning. Primarily nocturnal. Extremely aggressive when cornered.
snakes.facts: Responsible for the most snakebite fatalities in Asia. Venom causes massive internal bleeding and organ failure.

snakes.name: Common Krait
snakes.habitat: Cultivated Fields, Low Scrub Jungle, Human Settlements
snakes.behavior: Sluggish by day, highly active and dangerous at night. Bites sleeping victims on ground floors.
snakes.facts: Venom is 16× more potent than a cobra's. Bites are nearly painless initially, delaying medical treatment.

snakes.name: Saw-scaled Viper
snakes.habitat: Arid Desert, Rocky Terrain, Semi-arid Scrub
snakes.behavior: Produces loud rasping sound by rubbing coils together. Extremely quick strikes.
snakes.facts: Part of the 'Big Four' most dangerous snakes. Responsible for more human deaths than any other group of snakes.

snakes.name: Green Tree Python
snakes.behavior: Arboreal and nocturnal. Rests coiled on branches. Docile but can bite defensively.
snakes.facts: Young pythons are yellow or red, turning green as adults — convergent evolution with Amazon's emerald tree boa.

snakes.name: Indian Rat Snake
snakes.behavior: Fast-moving, excellent climber and swimmer. Often confused with cobras but completely harmless.
snakes.facts: Vital for pest control, consuming large numbers of rats and mice. Legally protected in many countries.

snakes.name: King Cobra
snakes.behavior: Can raise a third of its body off the ground and 'stand' 6ft tall. Growls rather than hisses.
snakes.facts: World's longest venomous snake, reaching 5.5m. Uniquely, it feeds almost exclusively on other snakes.

snakes.name: Ball Python
snakes.behavior: Docile and shy. Rolls into a tight ball when threatened — hence the name 'ball python'.
snakes.facts: The most popular pet snake in the world. Can live over 30 years in captivity.

snakes.name: Corn Snake
snakes.habitat: Overgrown Fields, Woodland Edges, Abandoned Buildings
snakes.behavior: Docile and slow-moving. An accomplished climber. Mainly nocturnal or crepuscular.
snakes.facts: Named for their corn-like belly pattern. One of the most popular pet snakes due to gentle temperament.
